name: Stir-Fried Bean Curd with Ground Pork and Oys
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon finely minced hot green chile
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the ground pork, beaten egg, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, five-spice powder, 2 tablespoons scallions, 1 tablespoon sugar, 3/4 tablespoon sesame oil, and a pinch of salt.
2. Place vegetable oil in a hot wok over high heat. Add small clumps of the pork mixture and stir-fry for about 1 minute, or until browned and cooked through. Remove from the wok and set aside.
3. Lower the heat to medium. Add the remaining scallions, minced red pepper, minced green chile, garlic, and ginger root to the wok. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
4. Add the browned pork and stir well, tossing with the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar.
5. Turn the heat high. Add chicken stock, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Stir well and bring to a boil.
6. Mix the remaining 2 teaspoons cornstarch with a little cold water in a cup. Stir this mixture into the wok, then gently stir in the bean curd cubes, taking care not to break them up.
7. Remove from the wok and place on a serving platter. Sprinkle with the remaining half-teaspoon of sesame oil.
Yield: 4 servings
